title: onobrychis sativa subsp. supina
intro: Onobrychis sativa subsp. supina, also known as hairy vetch, is a perennial legume plant that is native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high content of isoflavones and other compounds. The health benefits of hairy vetch include its ability to support cardiovascular health, reduce menopausal symptoms, and act as an antioxidant, while also providing relief from digestive issues and skin conditions. Hairy vetch is often prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits and promote overall well-being.
